sources.title: The Broadway Melody  (MGM, 1929) ANITA PAGE🍕 BESSIE LOVE🍕 OSCAR WINNER
sources.description:
PizzaFLIX Presents CLASSIC MOVIE MONDAY
Stars: Anita Page, Bessie Love, Charles King
Director: Harry Beaumont
🍕OSCAR WINNER BEST PICTURE🍕
0:00            PizzaFLIX Intro
00:52          The Broadway Melody 
35:31          Rehearsal "The Broadway Melody" number
41:22          Rehearsal “Love Boat”
01:13:42    “The Wedding Of The Painted Doll” (restored)
01:17:25    “The Boy Friend”

Step back into the Golden Age of Hollywood with The Broadway Melody (1929), the Best Picture Oscar Winner that revolutionized the movie musical! 

The dazzling Anita Page and Oscar-nominated Bessie Love are two ambitious sisters chasing their dreams on Broadway. Alongside Charles King, they navigate romance, rivalry, and showbiz struggles in this unforgettable backstage drama.

The Broadway Melody was a groundbreaking achievement directed by Harry Beaumont—the first full-length all-talking, all-singing musical and the first sound film to win an Academy Award! Featuring iconic early musical numbers like You Were Meant for Me and the title song The Broadway Melody, this cinematic milestone set the stage for MGM’s future dominance in the genre.

Experience the magic of classic Hollywood musicals, Pre-Code cinema, and the dazzling performances that made Anita Page, Bessie Love, and Charles King household names. Don’t miss this essential piece of film history—watch now on PizzaFLIX, your home for classic movies!

🎭 Subscribe for more Oscar-winning films, vintage musicals, and Pre-Code Hollywood gems!

Are you a PizzaFLIX Fanatic? Join our exclusive Fan Club for dedicated Classic movie lovers: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCE34fwgW7kWr7tc7YiOWtRw/join

metadata.Plot: Hank and Queenie Mahoney, a vaudeville act, come to Broadway, where their friend Eddie Kerns needs them for his number in one of Francis Zanfield's shows. Eddie was in love with Hank, but when he meets Queenie, he falls in love to her, but she is courted by Jock Warriner, a member of the New Yorker high society. It takes a while till Queenie recognizes, that she is for Jock nothing more than a toy, and it also takes a while till Hank recognizes that Eddie is in love with Queenie.
metadata.Writer: Edmund Goulding, Norman Houston, James Gleason
metadata.Actors: Bessie Love, Anita Page, Charles King
